ModuleName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured JSON outline listing all topics and subtopics covered in the module, including data quality dimensions, missing values, duplicate records, and outlier detection, used by instructors to plan content and by learners to navigate the module's scope.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A navigational overview of Module 3 topics listing and briefly describing the key subject areas covered—including data quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, reliability) and data cleaning fundamentals—that learners would use to preview or locate specific content within the module.

Artifacts.Name: Data_Quality_Assessment_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing and explaining data quality assessment and cleaning fundamentals — covering dimensions such as accuracy, completeness, and consistency, as well as missing values and duplicates — used by instructors to deliver lectures and by learners to review core concepts.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Data Quality
Artifacts.Description: A foundational reading explaining what data quality means, why raw real-world data is imperfect, and the core dimensions and consequences of poor data quality, used by learners to build conceptual grounding before tackling specific cleaning techniques.

Artifacts.Name: Identifying Missing Values
Artifacts.Description: A reading covering how to detect and characterise missing data in datasets — including patterns, types, and practical identification methods — used by learners to understand the scope of gaps before deciding how to handle them.

Artifacts.Name: Handling Duplicate Data
Artifacts.Description: A reading explaining what duplicate records are, why they occur, how to detect them, and strategies for resolving them, used by learners to understand the risks duplicates pose and how to remove or reconcile them systematically.

Artifacts.Name: Detecting and Managing Outliers
Artifacts.Description: A reading defining outliers, explaining how they arise, and covering detection techniques and management options such as removal, transformation, or retention, used by learners to make defensible decisions about anomalous data points.

Artifacts.Name: Resolving Data Inconsistencies
Artifacts.Description: A reading exploring how data inconsistencies — such as conflicting representations of the same entity across records or sources — arise and how to identify and resolve them, used by learners to standardise data before analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Systematic Data Cleaning Strategies
Artifacts.Description: A reading presenting a structured, multi-stage workflow for data cleaning that emphasises auditing before modifying, documenting decisions, and ensuring reproducibility, used by learners and instructors as a process framework tying individual cleaning techniques together.

Artifacts.Name: Preparing Clean Data for Analysis
Artifacts.Description: A reading covering the final preparation steps needed to make cleaned data analysis-ready — including validation of prior cleaning decisions, formatting, and readiness checks — used by learners as a capstone guide before handing data off for analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Preparing Clean Data for Analysis
Artifacts.Description: A reading covering the final preparation steps needed to make cleaned data analysis-ready — including validation of prior cleaning decisions, formatting, and readiness checks — used by learners as a capstone guide before handing data off for analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank testing learners' understanding of data quality definitions, dimensions such as accuracy, consistency, and reliability, and concepts like missing values and duplicates, used by instructors to assess comprehension of the module's key topics.

Artifacts.Name: Data Quality Assessment: Identifying and Classifying Data Quality Issues
Artifacts.Description: An analysis assignment in which learners examine a provided dataset to identify, classify, and document common data quality issues (such as missing values, duplicates, and inconsistencies), then propose corrective actions; instructors use it to assess students' ability to systematically evaluate real-world data quality problems.

Artifacts.Name: Crossword Puzzle
Artifacts.Description: An interactive crossword puzzle covering key data quality and data cleaning terminology, used by learners to self-test and reinforce their understanding of module vocabulary through an across-and-down word puzzle with automatic scoring.
